mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with >60% accuracy in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, which compresses profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive or neutral 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% year-over-year, or the predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) in high-inflation subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate cut implemented while inflation remains below 3% signals a preemptive easing cycle that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 65% of instances meeting the trigger condition, or the signal shows no directional edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness rather than cyclical adjustment, which suppresses consumer spending forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 in rolling 5-year windows, or positive returns dominate during divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained economic expansion that directly supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 15-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines coinciding with elevated federal funds rates indicate monetary policy is actively suppressing output, which triggers immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the mechanism fails to generate a significant negative coefficient in regime-filtered regressions.
